description: Production MongoDB observability dashboard for chrono-queue. Covers server health, query throughput, read-vs-write latency, slow query rate, lock utilization, connections, WiredTiger cache, replication lag trends, and index efficiency.

panels.title: MongoDB Command p99 Latency
panels.description: 99th percentile MongoDB driver command latency. Profile budgets: low-volume target p99 <120ms, high-volume target p99 <250ms. Panel thresholds are tuned to warn early (80ms/250ms).

panels.title: Current Connections
panels.description: Current active MongoDB connections. Yellow >80, Red >150.

panels.title: WiredTiger Cache Usage %
panels.description: WiredTiger cache fill percentage. Yellow >80%, Red >90%. High values risk cache pressure and disk I/O spikes.

panels.title: Replication Lag (max)
panels.description: Max replication lag across secondaries in seconds. Yellow >5s, Red >15s — SRE escalation threshold.

panels.title: Lock Utilization (%)
panels.description: Global lock active clients as a percentage of current MongoDB connections. Yellow >5%, Red >15% indicate increasing lock contention risk.

panels.title: Slow Query Ratio (>100ms, %)
panels.description: Percentage of MongoDB commands slower than 100ms. Ratio-based thresholding adapts to read-heavy or write-heavy traffic mixes. Yellow >1%, Red >3%.

panels.title: Opcounters — Insert/Query/Update/Delete/Getmore (ops/s)
panels.description: MongoDB operations per second by type. Sudden spikes in deletes or inserts are worth investigating.

panels.title: Replication Opcounters (ops/s)
panels.description: Replication oplog replay rates. These should track primary opcounters with a small lag.

panels.title: Connections — Current vs Available
panels.description: Current vs available connections. Available approaching 0 = connection exhaustion. Yellow >80 current, Red >150.

panels.title: Global Lock — Active Clients (readers / writers / total)
panels.description: Active clients holding global lock by readers and writers. Sustained high writers indicates write contention.

panels.title: WiredTiger Cache Usage % (bytes in cache / max configured)
panels.description: WiredTiger cache fill ratio. Yellow >80%, Red >90%. High fill risks eviction and increased disk I/O.

panels.title: WiredTiger Cache — Bytes In Cache vs Max Configured
panels.description: WiredTiger cache in bytes. Dirty bytes rising close to total indicates checkpoint pressure.

panels.title: WiredTiger Cache — Bytes Read/Write + Eviction Attempts Rate
panels.description: WiredTiger cache byte-throughput and eviction pressure. Rising eviction attempts with high cache fill indicates memory pressure and potential latency impact.

panels.title: Replication Lag per Member
panels.description: Replication lag per member in seconds. Yellow >5s warning, Red >15s critical.

panels.title: Replica Set Member State
panels.description: Replica set member states: 1=PRIMARY, 2=SECONDARY, 6=UNKNOWN, 8=DOWN. Sudden state changes require immediate investigation.

panels.title: Replication Lag Trend (max)
panels.description: Trend of the maximum secondary replication lag over time.

panels.title: MongoDB Driver Command Latency — p50/p90/p95/p99 (all commands)
panels.description: Driver latency percentiles over time. Profile guidance: low-volume p95/p99 targets ~40ms/120ms, high-volume ~80ms/250ms.

panels.title: Read vs Write Latency — p95
panels.description: 95th percentile read and write latency split by command families. Profile guidance: low-volume keep p95 <40ms, high-volume keep p95 <80ms (write p95 may run higher under burst load).

panels.title: Query Throughput — Reads vs Writes (ops/s)
panels.description: Read and write command throughput over time.

panels.title: Slow Query Ratio Over Time (read/write/total, %)
panels.description: Read/write/total percentage of commands above 100ms. Ratio-based view remains stable across changing command mix and traffic volume.

panels.title: Latency vs Connections (Correlation)
panels.description: Overlay of p95 latency and current connection count to expose correlation under load.

panels.title: Server-Side Query Latency (reads/writes/commands µs)
panels.description: Average server-side operation latency in microseconds from mongodb top.

panels.title: Index Efficiency — Scan vs Index Usage
Query Scan Rates — Index Keys vs Documents vs ScanAndOrder
panels.description: High scanned objects vs index keys ratio = collection scans. High scanAndOrder = sort without covering index.

panels.title: Index Hit Ratio (indexed scans / total scans)
panels.description: Fraction of scanned index keys relative to total scanned keys+objects. <0.7 is poor (red), 0.7-0.85 is warning, >=0.85 is healthy.

panels.title: Commands/s by Operation (Success vs Failed)
panels.description: Driver command throughput split by status. Red FAIL series need immediate investigation.

panels.title: Failed Commands Rate by Operation
panels.description: Failed MongoDB commands by operation type. Any sustained insert/update failures = data loss risk.

panels.title: MongoDB Reads/s by Command
panels.description: Successful read operation rates (find, findAndModify, getMore, count, distinct, aggregate).

panels.title: MongoDB Writes/s by Command
panels.description: Successful write operation rates (insert, update, delete, findAndModify, bulkWrite, replace).

title: MongoDB
